title: ranunculus aquatilis subsp. penicillatus
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. penicillatus, commonly known as water crowfoot, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin from irritation and infection. The health benefits of water crowfoot are believed to include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and even exhibiting antioxidant properties. Water crowfoot can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption and use.
